isolation.scope: Single isolated agent-browser session against the shared localhost:3001 app; only in-browser game state was mutated.

assertions.title: Promotion Dialog
assertions.reason: Pass — loading the supplied promotion FEN, moving the white pawn from e7 to e8, and selecting a promotion piece produced a modal with four white-piece choices, blocked other board interaction until a choice was made, and completed promotion correctly on the board.

steps.action: Open the hidden FEN loader, enter `6k1/4P3/8/8/8/8/8/6K1 w - - 0 1`, and load it
steps.expected: A deterministic promotion-ready position appears with a white pawn on e7 and kings on g8/g1
steps.observed: The board showed `black king on g8`, `white pawn on e7`, and `white king on g1`.

steps.action: Move the white pawn from e7 to e8
steps.expected: A promotion dialog appears with four promotion choices matching White
steps.observed: A promotion dialog appeared with four options rendered as `♕ queen`, `♖ rook`, `♗ bishop`, and `♘ knight`.

steps.action: Try another interaction while the dialog is open
steps.expected: The modal blocks other interaction until a promotion piece is chosen
steps.observed: Board squares were disabled in the snapshot, and a click on `New Game` was rejected with `Element "@e10" is blocked by another element` while the dialog remained open.

steps.expected: The pawn is replaced by the selected piece on e8 and the dialog closes
steps.observed: The dialog closed, the board showed `white knight on e8` instead of a pawn, and move history recorded `1. e8=N`.

stateComparison.modalBlocking: Board squares were disabled while the dialog was open, and toolbar click attempts were blocked by the modal overlay.

assertions.title: En Passant Removes Correct Pawn
assertions.reason: Pass — loading the supplied en-passant FEN and capturing from e5 to d6 removed the black pawn from d5 and left the white pawn on d6, matching en-passant rules.

steps.action: Enter `6k1/8/8/3pP3/8/8/8/6K1 w - d6 0 1` in the FEN loader and load it
steps.expected: The board shows a black pawn on d5, a white pawn on e5, and an en-passant target on d6
steps.observed: The board showed `black king on g8`, `black pawn on d5`, `white pawn on e5`, and `white king on g1`.

steps.action: Move the white pawn from e5 to d6
steps.expected: The white pawn lands on d6 and the black pawn from d5 is removed
steps.observed: After the move, the board no longer contained a black pawn on d5 and showed `white pawn on d6`; the engine then quickly replied with `black king on f7`.

stateComparison.removedPiece: The black pawn that began on d5 was absent after the capture.
stateComparison.capturingPawn: The white pawn moved diagonally from e5 to d6 as expected for en passant.
stateComparison.note: The engine replied quickly after the player's move, but the captured post-move board still clearly showed the correct en-passant result.

frictions.description: The engine replied quickly after the en-passant capture, so the post-capture screenshot already included Black's response move.
frictions.resolution: Used the before/after piece-state comparison to confirm that the black pawn from d5 was removed and the white pawn remained on d6 despite the immediate reply.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. Promotion displayed a blocking four-choice white promotion dialog and completed correctly after selection; en passant removed the pawn from d5 and left the white pawn on d6, with no console or runtime errors observed.
